channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: San Francisco Driver Gets Probation After Killing a Family of 4
item.description: Mary Fong Lau, 80, will perform 200 hours of community service as part of her sentence. Her speeding vehicle crashed into a family as they waited for a bus ride to the zoo in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight
item.media:credit: Jessica Christian/San Francisco Chronicle, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Mary Fong Lau at the Hall of Justice in San Francisco in February.

item.title: Striking Down Pentagon Press Limits, Judge Vindicates Independent Journalism
item.description: The ruling cut deeper than left-versus-right politics, declaring that the policy imposed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Reporters carried their belongings as they left the Pentagon after turning in their press passes last year.

item.title: 700 Dogs and Cats Are Rescued in Animal Cruelty Raid in Los Angeles County
item.description: Animal control officers responded to a pet-adoption facility in Lake Hughes, Calif., after receiving complaints about neglect and overcrowding. No charges have been filed.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

item.title: A Look at the Escalating Battle for the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: There are several ways the United States could attempt to reopen the waterway, all of which carry substantial risks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The village of Kumzar, along the Strait of Hormuz in Oman.

item.title: Fresno Took 30 Years to Name Cesar Chavez Blvd. Undoing It Took a Day.
item.description: The reckoning for the labor leader’s sex abuse was swift in Fresno, Calif., the heart of the Central Valley region where the farmworker movement was born. Towns across the nation face similar debates.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

item.title: California Could Elect a Republican Governor Thanks to the ‘Jungle Primary’
item.description: The state’s wide-open “jungle primary” has Democrats on edge.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:description: From left, Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, Steve Hilton, a Republican, and Matt Mahan, a Democrat, are part of a large field in the California governor’s race.

item.title: U.S. Coast Guard Recovers One Man and Two Dead Bodies From Pacific Boat Strike Area
item.description: A spokesman for the Coast Guard did not directly connect the rescue operation to Thursday’s boat strike in the eastern Pacific Ocean. The vessel sent the man to Costa Rica, and recovered two dead bodies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )
item.media:credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A U.S. Coast Guard ship in San Juan, P.R., in December.

item.title: Justice Dept. Seeks to Drop Charges Against Officers in Breonna Taylor’s Death
item.description: The civil rights charges against the two Louisville, Ky., police officers stemmed from their involvement in drafting the no-knock search warrant that led to the fatal shooting.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.media:credit: Xavier Burrell for The New York Times
item.media:description: Breonna Taylor was killed by Louisville police officers during a no-knock raid in her apartment in 2020.

item.title: Mullin Said to Have Credited the King of Jordan for a Helicopter Rescue
item.description: The homeland security nominee brought up a mysterious trip in mid-2016 at a confirmation hearing. He visited Jordan that August, congressional records show.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: House Committee on Energy and Commerce
item.media:description: Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma has a history of suggesting that he has firsthand familiarity with overseas war-zone operations, despite having never served in the military.

item.title: What to Know About This Year’s Cherry Blossom Blooms
item.description: Spring, is that you? Where and when to find peak blooms in Washington, D.C., as well as New York, New Jersey, Oregon and Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:description: Though Tokyo’s cherry blossoms are starting to flower, admirers in Washington, D.C., and New York will have better luck seeing bright blooms in the final days of March and at the beginning of April.

item.title: Trump Officials Bypass Congress to Sell Weapons to U.A.E., Kuwait and Jordan
item.description: Some of the proposed sales, valued at more than $23 billion, were under review, while others were never sent to Congress. The administration is pushing them through without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio determined that “an emergency exists that requires the immediate sale” of weapons to the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Jordan, according to his department.

item.title: Hegseth Invokes Divine Purpose to Justify Military Might
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has imbued U.S. military actions with a Christian moral underpinning that suggests they are divinely sanctioned.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Elizabeth Dias
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has framed U.S. military actions as bigger than politics or U.S. foreign policy.

item.title: Presidents Who Honored Cesar Chavez Have Yet to React to Abuse Allegations
item.description: Bill Clinton gave Mr. Chavez the nation’s highest civilian honor. Barack Obama made Cesar Chavez Day a federal holiday. Joe Biden displayed a bronze bust of Mr. Chavez. So far, all have been quiet.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:description: Joseph R. Biden Jr. not only put the bust of Mr. Chavez in the Oval Office, but also gave it a place of prominence behind the Resolute Desk.

item.title: Cal State Challenges Trump’s Order on Transgender Student Athletes
item.description: The California State University system joins a short list of universities fighting Trump orders in court. The Education Department has threatened to cut federal funds to San Jose State University.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder and Michael C. Bender
item.media:description: The Education Department has targeted several universities, including San Jose State, where transgender athletes had played on teams before President Trump returned to the White House last year.

item.title: In Reversal, Trump Reinstates Support For Representative Jeff Hurd
item.description: President Trump reinstated his endorsement of Representative Jeff Hurd of Colorado, just weeks after pulling it back.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: Representative Jeff Hurd, a first-term congressman from Colorado, was one of six House Republicans who joined most Democrats in supporting a largely symbolic measure in February to rescind tariffs that Mr. Trump had imposed on Canada last year.

item.title: White House Unveils A.I. Policy Aimed at Blocking State Laws
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday released new guidelines for federal legislation on the technology, recommending some safeguards for children and consumer protections for energy costs.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and David Sacks, the Trump administration’s A.I. and crypto czar, during a round-table event in the White House last year.

item.title: Educators, How Will You Teach Cesar Chavez’s Story After Abuse Allegations?
item.description: We want to hear from educators who teach about Mr. Chavez.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez
item.media:description: A mural of Cesar Chavez being covered at Santa Ana College in Santa Ana, Calif., on Thursday.

item.title: A Meatpacking Strike in Colorado Is Another Stress to Trump’s Economy
item.description: The walkout at the JBS plant in Greeley, Colo., comes as beef prices have soared, cattle supplies have plunged and immigration sweeps have stressed the labor supply.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy and Daniel Brenner
item.category: United Food and Commercial Workers Union
item.media:description: Carmelo Garcia and other workers participated in the JBS meatpacking strike outside the JBS plant in Greeley, Colo. on Wednesday.

item.title: Deferring to Trump, G.O.P. Lawmakers Resist a Public Accounting on Iran
item.description: Nearly three weeks into a war that polls show is unpopular, top Republicans have yet to call administration officials to testify about it, arguing that hearings would put divisions on display.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, center, and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at an Oval Office meeting on Thursday. Democrats have demanded they make the case under oath in congressional hearings for the war.

item.title: Trump Friend Asked ICE to Detain the Mother of His Child
item.description: Paolo Zampolli, a former modeling agent and a longtime Trump ally, was in a custody battle over his son. An ICE official agreed to help.
item.dc:creator: Megan Twohey, Shawn McCreesh and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: Supreme Court Allows Street Preacher’s Lawsuit
item.description: Gabriel Olivier was arrested after violating an ordinance restricting demonstrations outside an amphitheater in Brandon, Miss.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Gabriel Olivier at the Supreme Court in 2025.

item.title: Trump Administration Sues Harvard Over Accusations of Antisemitism
item.description: The administration had spent months investigating the Ivy League school. The two sides had been in talks to negotiate a settlement.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder, Mark Arsenault and Michael C. Bender
item.media:credit: Lucy Lu for The New York Times
item.media:description: The government blocked billions of dollars in research funding for the school last year.

item.title: U.S. Military Ramps Up Assaults on Iranian Drones and Vessels to Clear Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Warplanes and attack helicopters are “hunting and killing” Iran’s fast-attack watercraft in contested sea lanes, Gen. Dan Caine said.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Cargo ships sailing toward the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of the United Arab Emirates on Thursday.

item.title: How a Civil Rights Icon Used His Power to Abuse Girls and Women
item.description: For five years, our reporters Manny Fernandez and Sarah Hurtes followed allegations that the civil rights icon Cesar Chavez had sexually abused girls and women at all levels of the United Farm Workers movement. Manny Fernandez takes us inside their investigation.
item.dc:creator: Manny Fernandez, Sarah Hurtes, Mimi Dwyer, Christina Shaman, Thomas Vollkommer, Jon Miller, James Surdam, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Chris Orr, Pedro Garcia and Rolando Garcia

item.title: ICE Released Hundreds of Children from Immigration Detention
item.description: About 50 children were in federal detention in Dilley, Texas this week, down from about 500 in January. It is unclear how many were deported, but some are back at their U.S. schools.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Mervosh, Miriam Jordan and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:description: The number of immigrant children in detention at the Dilley Immigration Processing Center in Texas has fallen sharply from earlier this year.

item.title: New Data Shows Where ICE Has Been Most Active This Year
item.description: The pace of ICE arrests nationwide has topped 1,100 per day on average in 2026, but the rate of arrests has varied across the country in sometimes surprising ways.
item.dc:creator: Albert Sun, Allison McCann and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: Pregnant in ICE Detention: Handcuffs and Pleas for Medical Care
item.description: Women describe conditions that violate longstanding agency guidelines for how pregnant detainees should be treated.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Kitchener, Charo Henríquez and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Natalie Keyssar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ely Lopera showing her husband a pregnancy test while on a video call from an ICE detention center.
